commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor or maintainer revision plus complete real behavior proof; automation should not queue a repair while proof is incomplete and the remaining blockers touch exec/network security boundaries.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a concrete network-boundary regression by sanitizing URLs inside the shared SSRF fetch guard.; Review finding: [P1] Keep URL sanitizing out of the shared fetch guard — src/in... (sha=12df98d385f081b4d293a1cf31a1c6c30fb298cb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds URL and shell-command sanitizers for leaked `<|...|>`-style tokens, wires them into `web_fetch`, the shared SSRF fetch guard, and bash exec, and adds sanitizer unit tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes bash exec commands through to the execution path and validates `web_fetch` URLs with `new URL`, so leading sentinel text can produce the reported shell or URL failure. I did not reproduce a live Gemma session in this review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer revision plus complete real behavior proof; automation should not queue a repair while proof is incomplete and the remaining blockers touch exec/network security boundaries.

target.title: fix(tools): sanitize LLM sentinel tokens in bash exec and web-fetch URL inputs
target.author: lisyoen
